scope: OpenCode subagent roster and routing policy

decision.rule: Delegate only when a bounded child adds independent context, parallel source discovery, or a useful read-only permission boundary. A specialist name alone is not evidence of capability.
decision.default_model_policy: Reviewed read-only roles inherit the invoking primary model unless the developer explicitly configures a role-specific override.
decision.interpretation: Configuration tests establish boundaries and resolution, not that invoking a role improves quality or total cost.

retained.trigger: A substantial independent writable slice whose ownership and file boundary are explicit; available only from Build and Ultra.
retained.boundary: Inherits the invoking controller model; no recursive delegation, Goal, advisor, interactive question, unknown external tool, or authority-requiring shell action.

retained.trigger: A bounded, independent repository-discovery question whose parallel execution materially reduces latency.
retained.boundary: Read-only; no shell, edits, recursive delegation, Goal, advisor, or interactive questions.

retained.trigger: A concrete consequential change with a supplied diff or exact changed-file boundary and intended behavior.
retained.boundary: Read-only; the parent supplies the diff or exact changed-file boundary; broad content search is denied and missing context is reported as unverified.

retained.trigger: A specific trust boundary or consequential authentication, authorization, secret, IPC, persistence, network, native-capability, or tool-egress change.
retained.boundary: Read-only; the parent supplies the trust boundary and evidence; broad content search is denied and a plausible exploit path is required.

retained.trigger: A durable decision that crosses component, data, ownership, concurrency, or deployment boundaries.
retained.boundary: Read-only; the parent supplies the decision boundary and current-system evidence; broad content search is denied.

retained.trigger: A concrete interface change where source or supplied runtime evidence supports a platform-specific accessibility review.
retained.boundary: Read-only; no broad search or commands; missing assistive-technology evidence remains unverified.

retained.trigger: A concrete query, schema, locking, migration, or rollout question with workload evidence.
retained.boundary: Read-only; no broad search or commands; executing diagnostics require a separately authorized workflow.

retained.trigger: Independent read-only analysis of an exact claim list and already-produced artifacts.
retained.boundary: No shell, interactive questions, edits, or delegation; controllers run deterministic checks and supply the results.

retired.reason: Generic and partly stale platform guidance, no local outcome evidence, and unsafe overlap with the production controller plus repository-local mobile instructions and skills.
A broad borrowed persona adds coordination prose without a distinct runtime capability; Ultra owns bounded native orchestration.
Broad writable persona duplicated the controller and lacked task-class evidence.
Broad writable persona duplicated the controller and applicable design skills without task-class evidence.
Repository instructions and Git skills provide the procedure; a persona added no distinct boundary.
Repository documentation guidance and the controller cover the task without an evidenced benefit from a separate persona.

hidden_command_only.request_boundary: exact files and a concrete question; content search is denied

configuration_evidence: Generated role declarations match their reviewed OpenCode-specific source prompts.
Runtime resolution tests enforce read, search, shell, edit, delegation, advisor, Goal, and unknown-tool boundaries.
Reviewed roles inherit the caller model by default and accept explicit machine-local model overrides.
The runtime validation environment is isolated from pre-existing global OpenCode state so stale model fields cannot masquerade as managed defaults.
Retired managed agents are absent after installation.
Subagents cannot invoke the interactive question tool; missing context must be returned as unverified.
The advisor and open-weight experiments are hidden and unavailable to automatic Task routing.
Open-weight command frontmatter pins exact model, provider, and serving-path routes while reusing the same reviewed permission boundary.
Known plugin-generated agents that their source package does not support on OpenCode are removed and rejected by post-install validation.
limitations: No matched production trial isolates the quality or total-cost effect of any retained specialist wrapper.
No authenticated matched Fireworks trial establishes open-weight provider quality, reliability, or realized OpenCode latency.
Model inheritance prevents an unmeasured provider switch but does not make a second same-model context free.
Source-only accessibility, security, database, and architecture review cannot substitute for runtime or service evidence.
A 100-step review-child limit bounds cost and runaway delegation; the developer can raise or remove it locally for an unusually large review. The writable general child is uncapped by default.
